sanskrit: अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः । गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ॥
hindiTranslation: सम्पूर्ण वृक्षोंमें पीपल, देवर्षियोंमें नारद, गन्धर्वोंमें चित्ररथ और सिद्धोंमें कपिल मुनि मैं हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Among all trees I am the sacred fig tree. Among divine sages I am Narada; among celestial musicians, Chitraratha; among perfected sages, the sage Kapila.
essence: The finest forms in creation are windows into Krishna.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna names the most revered example in each group of trees, sages, musicians, and perfected beings, showing how his presence appears through the finest forms in creation.
simpleInsight: The world is full of reminders. What you admire most can point you back to the divine.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna answers not with strategy but with vision. He continues listing the highest expressions of life — a sacred tree, Narada, Chitraratha, Kapila — so Arjuna can learn to recognize Krishna's presence everywhere.
modernRelevance: You are walking through a temple garden after a hard decision, and one tree, one song, one face suddenly steadies you. The world keeps offering small doorways back to what matters.
translationLiteral: अश्वत्थः (sacred fig tree) / सर्ववृक्षाणां (among all trees) / देवर्षीणां (among divine sages) / च (and) / नारदः (Narada) / गन्धर्वाणां (among celestial musicians) / चित्ररथः (Chitraratha) / सिद्धानां (among perfected beings) / कपिलः (Kapila) / मुनिः (sage)
insight: It trains the mind to notice excellence in the world as a pointer to the divine presence behind it.
